_description: Street-to-platform depth and estimated transit time per station/line for Madrid Metro. Data is scarce in public sources — individual station depths are mostly derived from engineering studies and blog posts. Line averages are inferred from gauge type, construction era, and known deep/shallow stations.
_methodology: Depth in metres refers to the platform rail level below street level. Transit time (seconds) is estimated assuming 1.2 m/s walking speed and 0.5 m/s escalator speed for vertical distance, plus horizontal walking time inside the station.

Cuatro Caminos.notes: L6 platform is the deepest in the entire Madrid Metro network. The tunnel between Cuatro Caminos and Guzmán el Bueno reaches 49 m — the absolute deepest point in the network.

Nuevos Ministerios.notes: Major interchange. Multiple deep platforms stacked vertically.

Moncloa.notes: Known as one of the deepest interchange stations. Incident with Serie 9000 train in 2007 at this station.

Sol.notes: Central hub. Relatively shallow for L1; deeper for L3.

Aluche.notes: At-grade surface station — one of the highest-elevation stations in the network. Minimal street-to-platform time.

L1.notes: Shallow narrow-gauge line. Oldest line (1919). Cut-and-cover construction in central sections.

L2.notes: Shallow narrow-gauge. Similar depth profile to L1.

L3.notes: Mostly shallow, but reaches significant depth at Moncloa.

L5.notes: Surface section (Eugenia de Montijo – Empalme). Otherwise shallow.

L6.notes: Deepest line in the network. Circular route required deep tunnelling under the city. Cuatro Caminos at 45 m.

L7.notes: Wide gauge. Surface sections in outer districts. Deep in central Madrid.

L8.notes: Modern airport line. Fewer stations, longer inter-station spacing.

L9.notes: Deepest urban sections in the network after L6. TFM suburban section is shallower.

L10.notes: Wide gauge. Surface in outer sections (Cuatro Vientos area). Deep in Nuevos Ministerios.

L11.notes: Short suburban line. Modern 1998 construction. Moderate depth.

L12.notes: Long suburban circular. Mix of surface and underground stations.

LR.notes: Very short line. Shallow historic narrow-gauge.

ML1.notes: Mostly surface. 4 surface + 5 underground stations, underground ones are shallow.

ML2.notes: Mostly surface tram. 10 surface + 3 underground stations.

simulation_recommendation.description: Suggested values for modeling Person transit time from street to platform in the simulation.
simulation_recommendation.notes: These are inferred averages. Applying a ±30% random variance per station gives a realistic spread. For simplicity in simulation, using a single network-wide value of 120 s (2 min) is reasonable.
